entities.name: positions.py
entities.context: Script at N5/scripts/positions.py for managing V's positions/beliefs, supports check-overlap, add, and extend commands

entities.name: N5
entities.context: V's personal knowledge/operations system located at /home/workspace/N5/

entities.name: Position System
entities.context: Framework for tracking V's beliefs and stances, with overlap checking, creation, and extension workflows

entities.name: Overlap Threshold
entities.context: Semantic similarity scoring system: ≥0.60 strong overlap (extend), 0.50-0.59 moderate (review), <0.50 low (create new)

entities.name: Stability Levels
entities.context: Position maturity classification, includes 'emerging' as one level for new positions

entities.name: Confidence Scoring
entities.context: Numeric confidence rating for positions, example value of 3 shown

entities.name: Domain
entities.context: Categorical grouping for positions within the position system

entities.name: Evidence Provenance
entities.context: Source tracking for positions supporting content_library, meeting, url, and conversation-based evidence types

entities.name: Content Library
entities.context: Referenced as an evidence source type (content_library:item-id) for position provenance

entities.name: check-overlap
entities.context: Subcommand of positions.py that checks semantic overlap between a new insight and existing positions

entities.name: Compound Insight
entities.context: Multi-component insight text composed of sub-claims, used when creating new positions

relationships.context: positions.py is a script within the N5 system at N5/scripts/
positions.py is the CLI implementation of the Position System
Threshold scoring determines whether to create new or extend existing positions
check-overlap is a subcommand of positions.py for semantic similarity checking
Stability classification (e.g. emerging) is a required field when creating positions
Confidence rating is a required numeric field when adding positions
Every position requires evidence sources traced to content library items, meetings, URLs, or conversations
Content library items are one of the accepted evidence source types for positions
Positions are built from compound insights decomposed into sub-claim components
Positions are organized by named domains as a primary categorization axis
